commands: Rewrite the first 2 seconds around the visible animal cue before publishing.
Keep one animal, one surprise, one payoff; remove second facts from weak scripts.
Put recovery categories through stricter hooks before they can publish.
Scale the categories already above 62% retention before adding new experiments.
Rewrite frame zero and the first spoken line around animal + real curiosity + visible anchor + payoff.
Reject packages with slow payoff, vague first-frame text, or medium/high swipe risk.
Use Studio Reach exports to replace this baseline with per-video stayed-to-watch data.
studio_reach.diagnosis: Stayed-to-watch is 31.8%, 8.2 points below the operating floor.

commands: Use a soft channel promise CTA only after the payoff, never before it.
Connect the CTA to the viewer identity: follow for one animal signal a day.
Apply the CTA only to strong candidates so weak videos do not waste the close.
Put the same channel promise after strong payoffs: Follow for one animal signal a day.
Publish recognizable lanes and sequels so new viewers know what returns tomorrow.
Treat a video as a full winner only when it drives retention plus subscribers, comments, or return signals.
audience_recurrence.diagnosis: New viewers are not yet becoming a repeat audience: subscribe gap 0.12 points, recurring gap 1.9 points.

prompts: Answer this viewer question: Watch the ocean song again. Tomorrow in Hidden Network: another animal signal. What feels more impossible: the ocean's depth, pressure, or creatures?
Answer this viewer question: Watch the tree rings again. Tomorrow in Hidden Network: another nature signal. Do trees feel more like structures, networks, or living machines?
Answer this viewer question: Watch the cool canopy again. Tomorrow in Hidden Network: another nature signal. Do forests feel more like places, systems, or living machines?
Answer this viewer question: Watch the cool canopy again. Tomorrow in Hidden Network: another nature signal. Do forests feel more like places, systems, or living machines?
Answer this viewer question: Watch the cool canopy again. Tomorrow in Hidden Network: another nature signal. Do forests feel more like places, systems, or living machines?
commands: Turn repeated viewer questions into the next queue refresh.

winner_rules: If retention is above 62% and growth beats 180, make a sequel before chasing a new topic.
If views are high but retention is below 62%, remake the hook instead of copying the topic.
If both views and retention are low, retire the angle for 14 days.

winners.title: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
Butterflies are covered in tiny color scales
Lightning turns air into a shock wave
Snakes smell the air with their tongues
Lions have dark ear marks cubs can follow

watch.title: Dogs read the world through scent maps
Deer aim their ears like sound radar
Horses sleep standing without falling over
Ducks can act injured to pull danger away

identity.brand_promise: Wild Brief decodes one surprising animal signal in a fast, human, evidence-aware Short.
voice_rules: Open with the animal and the payoff inside the first sentence.
Use concrete body cues viewers can see on screen.
End with a calm promise, not a hard sell.
forbidden_patterns: Two unrelated facts in one Short.
Overusing words like secret, amazing or incredible.
CTA before the viewer gets the payoff.
identity.cta_formula: Follow for one animal signal a day.

lanes.promise: home-animal behavior with stricter retention gates

commands: Name each new script internally by series so learning compounds.
Publish in repeatable lanes, not random animals.
Keep one experimental lane open so the channel can discover the next breakout.

review_questions: Which category creates both views and retention?
Which narrator and hook style should become default?
Which topics must be paused for 14 days?

objective.name: Turn Shorts Feed discovery into retained viewers, subscribers, and recurring audience.
objective.source: YouTube Studio screenshots plus authenticated Studio read by Codex

operating_principle: Scale only what earns stayed-to-watch, subscribers, and return signals.
